findings.message: Focus outline is removed (`outline: none`) but the design system never adopts `:focus-visible` — keyboard users may lose the focus indicator
findings.suggestion: scope outline removal to `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` and provide a visible `:focus-visible` style (or adopt the `focus-visible` polyfill)

findings.message: Design system paints colors but defines no forced-colors / high-contrast handling — in Windows High Contrast Mode (forced-colors) components may lose borders, focus rings, and meaning conveyed by color
findings.suggestion: add a `@media (forced-colors: active)` block (use `forced-color-adjust` and system color keywords for borders/focus), or a `prefers-contrast` / high-contrast token set

findings.message: No agent instruction files found (neither `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` nor `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`)
findings.suggestion: add at least one Cursor rule under `.cursor/rules/` or one Claude skill under `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` so coding agents have project-specific guidance

findings.message: No MCP config file found — design system is not declared as AI-agent-accessible
findings.suggestion: create `.mcp.json` (Claude Code), `.cursor/mcp.json` (Cursor), or `claude_desktop_config.json` declaring at least one MCP server with a `command`

findings.message: AGENTS.md has no fenced code block starting with a runnable shell command (pnpm/npm/python/...)
findings.suggestion: add a section with a fenced code block whose first line is a real command an agent can run

findings.message: AGENTS.md does not reference exit codes — agents cannot verify command success programmatically
findings.suggestion: document expected exit codes (e.g., `exit code 0 = clean`) for the commands you list

findings.message: AGENTS.md does not mention any toolchain config file present in the repo (package.json, tsconfig.json, ...)
findings.suggestion: reference the project's actual config files so agents know which toolchain is in scope

findings.message: No structured CHANGELOG found — AI agents (and humans) can't see what changed between versions
findings.suggestion: add a CHANGELOG.md with version-structured entries (Keep a Changelog: `## [1.2.0]` headings) so consumers and AI agents can track changes and breaking updates

findings.message: 4 files contain an AI output or streaming component with no live region — screen-reader users will not hear streamed content: code-connect/AILabel/AILabel.figma.tsx, code-connect/AILabel/AILabelActions.figma.tsx, code-connect/AILabel/AILabelContent.figma.tsx, src/components/Tile/Tile.tsx. Wrap each output in aria-live="polite", role="status", or PatternFly isLiveRegion.
findings.suggestion: Add aria-live="polite" (or role="status") to the container wrapping the AI output component, or use PatternFly's isLiveRegion prop.

findings.message: [jsx-a11y/control-has-associated-label] A control must be associated with a text label.
No llms.txt at repo root — AI agents have no top-level map of the design system
findings.suggestion: add an llms.txt file (llmstxt.org spec) with an H1 title, a blockquote summary, and `## <section>` link lists

findings.message: package.json 'types' points to 'lib/index.d.ts' which does not exist — build output is missing or path is wrong
findings.suggestion: run the package build, or update the 'types' field to point at the produced .d.ts file

findings.message: Hardcoded font-weight `600` — typography should come from a type token scale
findings.suggestion: reference a typography token (e.g. `--font-size-md`, `--font-weight-semibold`) instead of a raw value

<label> has a click handler but no role — keyboard and screen-reader users can't operate it (use <button>, or add role + tabIndex + a key handler)
findings.suggestion: replace <label onClick> with a <button>, or add role="button" tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler

findings.message: [jsx-a11y/control-has-associated-label] A control must be associated with a text label.
Bare `@deprecated` tag with no migration guidance — a coding agent reading this symbol can't tell what to use instead
findings.suggestion: add a replacement pointer to the `@deprecated` tag, e.g. `@deprecated Use {@link NewButton} instead` or a `@see` sibling tag, so consumers and AI agents can migrate automatically

findings.message: Bare `@deprecated` tag with no migration guidance — a coding agent reading this symbol can't tell what to use instead
findings.suggestion: add a replacement pointer to the `@deprecated` tag, e.g. `@deprecated Use {@link NewButton} instead` or a `@see` sibling tag, so consumers and AI agents can migrate automatically

findings.message: [jsx-a11y/control-has-associated-label] A control must be associated with a text label.
Inline `<svg>` has no `viewBox` — a fixed-size icon without a viewBox does not scale and can crop
findings.suggestion: add a `viewBox` (e.g. `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`) so the icon scales cleanly at any size; keep width/height for the default render size

findings.message: Hardcoded font-size `14px` — typography should come from a type token scale
findings.suggestion: reference a typography token (e.g. `--font-size-md`, `--font-weight-semibold`) instead of a raw value

findings.message: <div> has a click handler but no role — keyboard and screen-reader users can't operate it (use <button>, or add role + tabIndex + a key handler)
findings.suggestion: replace <div onClick> with a <button>, or add role="button" tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler

findings.message: [jsx-a11y/control-has-associated-label] A control must be associated with a text label.
Inline <svg> has no accessible treatment — a decorative icon should be `aria-hidden`, and a meaningful one needs `role="img"` + a label
findings.suggestion: add `aria-hidden="true"` if the icon is decorative, or `role="img" aria-label="…"` (or a <title> child) if it conveys meaning

findings.message: <span> has a click handler but no role — keyboard and screen-reader users can't operate it (use <button>, or add role + tabIndex + a key handler)
findings.suggestion: replace <span onClick> with a <button>, or add role="button" tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler

findings.message: Inline `<svg>` has no `viewBox` — a fixed-size icon without a viewBox does not scale and can crop
findings.suggestion: add a `viewBox` (e.g. `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`) so the icon scales cleanly at any size; keep width/height for the default render size

findings.message: Inline `<svg>` has no `viewBox` — a fixed-size icon without a viewBox does not scale and can crop
findings.suggestion: add a `viewBox` (e.g. `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`) so the icon scales cleanly at any size; keep width/height for the default render size

findings.message: Inline `<svg>` has no `viewBox` — a fixed-size icon without a viewBox does not scale and can crop
findings.suggestion: add a `viewBox` (e.g. `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`) so the icon scales cleanly at any size; keep width/height for the default render size

findings.message: <div> has a click handler but no role — keyboard and screen-reader users can't operate it (use <button>, or add role + tabIndex + a key handler)
findings.suggestion: replace <div onClick> with a <button>, or add role="button" tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler

findings.message: AI surface detected but no named AI loading state with paired text found — a bare spinner is not sufficient (AWS Cloudscape gen-AI: loading states must carry visible text, e.g. "Generating response…").
findings.suggestion: Add a named component such as Generating, Thinking, AILoading, or StreamingIndicator that always renders a visible/accessible status string alongside the spinner.

findings.message: AI surface detected but no AI-specific error state found — generic error boundaries do not communicate AI-specific failure context.
findings.suggestion: Add a component such as AIError or GenerationError that clearly communicates the AI operation failed and what the user can do next.

findings.message: An AI-marker component is present but no non-human identity label was detected (HAX G1 / PAIR "Set expectations"). Ship a bot/AI avatar or persona label so users know they are not talking to a human.
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated non-human identity affordance (e.g. AiAvatar, BotAvatar, AssistantAvatar, AiPersona, NonHumanBadge) on conversational AI surfaces.

findings.message: An AI-marker component is present but no confidence/uncertainty indicator was detected (HAX G2 / PAIR Trust). Ship a component that communicates how certain the AI output is so users can calibrate trust.
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated confidence affordance (e.g. ConfidenceBadge, ConfidenceScore, UncertaintyIndicator, CertaintyMeter) shown alongside AI-generated content.

findings.message: An AI-marker component is present but no feedback control was detected (HAX G15 / PAIR Feedback). Ship a thumbs-up/down, rating, or helpful/unhelpful component so users can signal AI output quality.
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated feedback component (e.g. AiFeedback, ThumbsUp/ThumbsDown, StarRating, WasThisHelpful) and optionally expose a reason enum (inaccurate, unhelpful, offensive) for categorized feedback.

findings.message: An AI surface is present but no AI interaction-pattern documentation was detected (Kavcic). Document how AI suggestion, generation, authorization, handoff, regeneration, and history patterns work in this design system.
findings.suggestion: Add in-repo pattern docs (e.g. `docs/ai-patterns.md`) with headings covering the AI interaction types: suggestion, generation, authorization, handoff, regeneration, history.

findings.message: An AI-marker component is present but no source-attribution component was detected (HAX G11 / PAIR Explainability). Ship a citation/attribution component so AI-generated answers can reference their sources.
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated attribution affordance (e.g. Citation, Citations, SourceAttribution, Provenance) shown alongside AI-generated content.

findings.message: No theme-mode signal found — design system does not appear to define light/dark modes
findings.suggestion: add a `prefers-color-scheme` media query, a `[data-theme]` selector, a `.dark`/`.light` class convention, or a DTCG token group named `dark`/`light`

findings.message: No component manifest JSON found — MCP tools fall back to source-file reads
findings.suggestion: create a `components.json` or `lyse.components.json` listing your components ({ name, sourceFile }) so MCP servers can serve component lookups at ~5× lower cost than reading source files

findings.message: AI-marker components detected: AILabel, AILabel.figma, AILabelActions, AILabelActions.figma, AILabelContent, AILabelContent.figma

findings.message: AI draft-attribution convention detected ("first draft created with [tool]" / structured AI-generated marker). (Appendix A)
findings.suggestion: Attribution convention found — keep applying it consistently to AI-assisted content so provenance is transparent.
